stem: The area of a rectangle is 96 square inches. The length of the longest side of the rectangle is 24 inches. What is the length, in inches, of the shortest side of this rectangle?
rationale: The area of a rectangle equals length times width. With area 96 and longest side 24, the shortest side x satisfies 96 = 24x. Dividing by 24 gives x = 4 inches.
